calendar.matters:
MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Appointment Of A Reciever And To Appoint Arthur N. Harris As Receiver
Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association's motion for appointment of a receiver and to appoint Arthur N. Harris as receiver is granted. No opposition filed. ( Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =302/HEK

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2011 Off Calendar

Notice And Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Sep-29-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day Motion for Award of Attorney's Fees by Plaintiff.
Dept. 514, Judge: ELLEN CHAITIN, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha , CSR: B. FIELDS, #7235
Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. special appearance for Plaintiff Creditors Trade Assoc., Inc. dba Great Western Collection Bureau.
After the Court heard testimony by counsel and considered of the moving papers, granted an order and awarded $3,100.00 for attorney's fees, signing an order for same in open court.

Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-16-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: J. Willis-McGhee,
CSR: B. Fields, CSR #7235

Stephen Kappos, Esq. appeared for plaintiff(s) Creditor's Trade Bureau, Inc,
The Court considered Plaintiff's Written Declaration
Plaintiff's witness Elizabeth MacDOnald Sworn and examined.
Default has been entered against the Defendant(s).

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) Brelia Enterprises, Inc for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $28,593.38
Prejudgment Interest - $1,522.80
Costs - $605.00
Total judgment in the amount of $30,721.18
Attorneys fees to be determined by Motion.
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
